commentary_text: The starkest gap lies on Germany to win: the market prices it at 63% while the model estimates 36%, a divergence of 27 percentage points in the market's favor. The model rates this matchup as nearly even across all three outcomes, but sportsbooks and crypto markets have converged on much heavier German odds. This widening confidence could reflect real-time information unavailable to the model—recent form in qualifying or friendlies, lineup composition changes, or injury status among key players—that systematically favors Germany. Alternatively, the market may be pricing in structural factors the statistical model underweights. The dashboard surfaces this gap as a signal of where informed money and latest intelligence diverge from pure Elo-based forecasting; it does not adjudicate which source better captures match reality.
